categories.name: Security & Privacy
categories.description: Data handling, privacy mode requirements, compliance certifications, and code retention policies

low: Schedule a security review with your CISO team and share Cursor's SOC 2 Type II report
Document data classification requirements for code and define acceptable AI usage policies
Enable Privacy Mode in pilot configuration and validate zero data retention with your security team
medium: Complete vendor security questionnaire (VSA/SIG Lite) with Cursor's trust documentation
Define which repositories are in-scope for AI assistance based on data sensitivity tiers
Establish audit logging requirements and confirm admin dashboard visibility meets your needs
high: Fast-track enterprise agreement with DPA and custom security addendum if needed
Publish internal AI coding policy referencing Cursor's privacy controls as approved standard
Integrate Cursor usage into existing security monitoring and incident response playbooks

questions.text: Does your organization require SOC 2 Type II or equivalent compliance from AI coding tool vendors?
What is your policy on code/data retention by third-party AI services?
Have you classified which codebases contain regulated or sensitive data (PII, PHI, PCI)?

categories.name: Admin Controls
categories.description: Centralized administration, usage policies, team management, and audit capabilities

low: Identify IT/admin owners who will manage the Cursor enterprise dashboard
Define team structure mapping (org units, cost centers, pilot cohorts)
Review Cursor admin docs for usage limits, model restrictions, and member management
medium: Configure admin roles and delegate team leads for pilot cohort management
Set usage policies and spending caps aligned with your AI governance framework
Establish monthly admin review cadence for usage analytics and policy compliance
high: Integrate Cursor admin reporting into existing IT service management workflows
Automate provisioning/deprovisioning via SCIM alongside admin dashboard policies
Define enterprise-wide configuration standards for model access and feature toggles

questions.text: Do you require centralized admin control over AI tool configuration and usage?
Can you enforce usage limits, model restrictions, or feature toggles org-wide?
Do you need audit logs of AI tool usage for compliance reporting?

categories.name: SSO & Provisioning
categories.description: Single sign-on integration, SCIM provisioning, and identity lifecycle management

low: Confirm your IdP (Okta, Azure AD, etc.) supports SAML/OIDC SSO with Cursor
Identify identity team contacts for SSO configuration and test user provisioning
Plan pilot with manual invites first, SSO as a phase-2 milestone
medium: Configure SSO integration in staging and validate group-to-team mapping
Test JIT provisioning flow with a small pilot group before broader rollout
Document offboarding process to revoke access when developers leave
high: Enable SCIM auto-provisioning tied to your IdP groups and cost centers
Integrate Cursor license allocation into existing software request workflows
Automate deprovisioning as part of standard HR offboarding runbooks

questions.text: Is SSO (SAML/OIDC) a mandatory requirement for developer tools?
Do you need automated user provisioning/deprovisioning (SCIM)?
How mature is your identity and access management for dev tools?

categories.name: Repo Governance
categories.description: Source control policies, repository access, .cursorignore rules, and codebase indexing boundaries

low: Inventory repositories in scope for the pilot and tag by sensitivity level
Define .cursorignore patterns for secrets, credentials, and excluded directories
Align with platform team on GitHub/GitLab Enterprise integration requirements
medium: Establish repo allowlist/blocklist policy for AI-assisted development
Configure codebase indexing boundaries and validate no secrets are indexed
Create internal guidelines for which repos are approved for Cursor Agent mode
high: Integrate repo governance into existing code ownership and CODEOWNERS policies
Automate .cursorignore templates across monorepos and standard project scaffolds
Enable org-wide rules for repository access synced with your source control ACLs

questions.text: Do you have policies governing which repositories can use AI coding assistance?
How do you prevent AI tools from indexing secrets or sensitive files?
What source control platform does your engineering org primarily use?

categories.name: Model Governance
categories.description: AI model selection, usage policies, approved model lists, and third-party model risk management

low: Review Cursor's available models and your organization's AI model usage policy
Identify which model providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) are approved by your AI governance team
Define a pilot policy restricting model access to approved models only
medium: Configure admin-level model restrictions aligned with your approved vendor list
Document model selection guidelines for different task types (Agent vs. Tab completion)
Establish a process for requesting new model approvals through AI governance
high: Integrate Cursor model policies into enterprise AI governance framework
Set up monitoring for model usage patterns and cost allocation by team
Publish internal best practices for model selection by use case and sensitivity

questions.text: Does your organization maintain an approved list of AI models/providers?
Can you restrict which AI models developers access org-wide?
Have you assessed third-party model risk (data handling, output liability)?

categories.name: Adoption & Change Management
categories.description: Champion programs, training plans, communication strategy, and resistance mitigation

low: Identify 5�8 developer champions across different teams and tech stacks
Create a change management plan with communication timeline and training schedule
Run a small proof-of-concept (10�20 devs) before expanding the pilot
medium: Launch a champion program with weekly office hours and internal Slack/Teams channel
Develop role-based enablement content (Agent workflows, Tab completion, codebase context)
Establish feedback loops: bi-weekly surveys and monthly champion syncs
high: Scale champion network to cover all major engineering teams and geographies
Integrate Cursor training into onboarding for all new engineering hires
Publish internal case studies and productivity metrics to drive organic adoption

questions.text: Do you have a structured change management process for new developer tools?
Have you identified developer champions to drive adoption?
How do you plan to measure and communicate adoption progress?

categories.name: Developer Workflow Fit
categories.description: IDE compatibility, existing toolchain integration, language support, and developer experience alignment

low: Survey developers on current IDE usage (VS Code, JetBrains, etc.) and pain points
Run a 2-week workflow assessment with 5�10 developers across different stacks
Identify integration requirements (linters, formatters, CI/CD, monorepo tooling)
medium: Validate Cursor compatibility with your top 3 language/framework combinations
Document migration path from current IDE for developers willing to switch
Test Agent mode workflows against your most common development tasks
high: Standardize Cursor as primary IDE with VS Code fallback policy
Create internal workflow playbooks for Agent, Tab, and codebase indexing patterns
Integrate Cursor into your developer platform golden paths and templates

questions.text: What percentage of your developers use VS Code as their primary IDE?
How well does your codebase structure support AI context (monorepos, docs, tests)?
Are developers open to adopting AI-assisted workflows?

options.label: Strong demand / already using AI tools

categories.name: Executive Sponsorship
categories.description: C-level support, budget authority, strategic alignment, and organizational buy-in

low: Build an executive business case with ROI projections and competitive landscape
Identify a VP-level sponsor in Engineering or IT to champion the initiative
Schedule introductory briefing with CTO/CIO on AI-assisted development trends
medium: Secure budget allocation for a structured 90-day pilot with defined success criteria
Align Cursor initiative with top 3 engineering priorities on the executive roadmap
Establish quarterly business reviews with executive sponsor to track progress
high: Position Cursor as a strategic initiative with board-level visibility on AI productivity
Integrate adoption targets into engineering OKRs and leadership scorecards
Fast-track enterprise procurement with executive-signed business justification

questions.text: Is there active executive sponsorship for AI-assisted development tools?
Is budget allocated or allocatable for an enterprise AI coding tool?
Does this initiative align with stated engineering or business priorities?

readinessLevels.recommendation: Significant groundwork needed. Prioritize executive sponsorship, security policy definition, and a small proof-of-concept before committing to a formal pilot.
nextSteps: Secure a VP-level executive sponsor and schedule a CTO/CIO briefing
Complete security and privacy policy review with your CISO team
Run a 2-week workflow assessment with 5�10 developers
Identify 3�5 champions and define a minimal viable pilot scope

readinessLevels.recommendation: Foundation is forming but gaps remain. Address your lowest-scoring categories before expanding beyond a controlled pilot.
nextSteps: Close gaps in your two lowest-scoring categories (see category recommendations)
Configure SSO and admin controls for the pilot cohort
Establish repo governance rules and model usage policies
Launch a structured 60�90 day pilot with defined success metrics

readinessLevels.recommendation: Strong foundation for enterprise adoption. Proceed with a structured pilot and begin procurement conversations in parallel.
nextSteps: Launch a 90-day pilot with 10�15% of engineering org
Enable SSO/SCIM and admin policies before scaling beyond pilot
Begin MSA/procurement process with legal and vendor management
Establish quarterly business reviews with executive sponsor

readinessLevels.recommendation: Organization is well-prepared for enterprise rollout. Proceed with procurement, MSA finalization, and phased deployment planning.
nextSteps: Finalize enterprise agreement and procurement timeline
Create phased rollout plan for remaining engineering teams
Integrate Cursor into developer onboarding and platform golden paths
Establish ongoing governance cadence and ROI tracking dashboard
